business.name: Elite Concrete
business.serviceArea: Bainbridge Island & North Seattle — Ballard, Magnolia, Queen Anne, Fremont, Wallingford, Phinney Ridge, Green Lake
business.about: A concrete flatwork and decorative concrete studio serving Bainbridge Island and the north Seattle neighborhoods. Proper base preparation, reinforced pours, and Seattle drainage planning — done right the first time so it doesn't crack, heave, or drain toward your foundation.

services.name: Driveway
services.description: Demolition, excavation, compacted gravel base, rebar or wire-mesh reinforcement, and a properly sloped pour. Control joints placed to manage cracking, not hide it. Brushed, broom, or exposed-aggregate finish.

services.name: Patio & Walkways
services.description: Outdoor living surfaces poured with drainage grade away from the house — a Seattle non-negotiable. Brushed, broom, exposed aggregate, or decorative border options.

services.name: Foundation & Footings
services.description: Footings for additions, decks, garages, and ADUs poured to Washington frost depth. Engineer-stamped designs accepted and coordinated with permit requirements.

services.name: Stamped & Decorative
services.description: Stamped patterns, color integral or acid stain, and exposed aggregate finishes. We set expectations honestly — stamped concrete is beautiful but requires sealing every 2–3 years in PNW conditions.

services.name: Retaining Wall
services.description: Cast-in-place concrete retaining walls with proper drainage aggregate, weep holes, and rebar schedule for your specific soil and height conditions.

services.name: Repair & Resurfacing
services.description: Crack repair, surface grinding, and microtop or overlay resurfacing for driveways and patios that are structurally sound but cosmetically aged.

testimonials.name: Steve L.
testimonials.text: Previous driveway cracked in three spots within two winters. Elite Concrete spent two hours just on base prep before pouring. That was four years ago — still no cracks.

testimonials.name: Amanda K.
testimonials.text: Stamped patio looked incredible and they were upfront about the sealing schedule. No surprises. The drainage grade was something the other bidder didn't even mention.

testimonials.name: Phil R.
testimonials.text: Retaining wall on a steep slope. They submitted the rebar schedule to our structural engineer before pouring. That's the standard I expected — not everyone delivers it.

testimonials.name: Karen H.
testimonials.text: New walkway and steps from the driveway to the front door. Clean pour, good texture, and perfectly graded away from the house. Job site was spotless every evening.

process.title: Assess
process.description: We visit the site, evaluate soil conditions, drainage, and existing concrete, then deliver a written scope with material and reinforcement spec — not just a price.

process.title: Prepare
process.description: Demo, excavation, and base preparation are where a concrete job is won or lost. We compact the sub-base in lifts, set drainage grade, and place reinforcement before a truck rolls up.

process.title: Pour
process.description: Proper mix design for PNW conditions, control joints placed at correct spacing, and a finish texture suited to foot or vehicle traffic. No troweling over bleed water.

process.title: Cure
process.description: Curing compound applied immediately after finishing. We don't walk away at pour day — we monitor the slab through the cure window and return for final joint sealing.

chatbot.greeting: Hi — Elite Concrete's project estimator. Tell me about the concrete work you need and I'll give you a ballpark and next steps.
qualifyingQuestions: What type of concrete work are you looking at — driveway, patio, foundation, or something else?
Is this new pour or repair/replacement of existing concrete?
Roughly what size is the area in square feet?
chatbot.customInstructions: Anchor on base prep quality, proper drainage slope, reinforcement specification, and Seattle freeze-thaw and rain considerations. Reference honest expectations on decorative concrete maintenance. Steer large projects toward an on-site assessment before quoting.

persona.name: Olivia
persona.title: Project Estimator
persona.proactiveBubbleText: Hi — driveway, patio, or foundation work? I can give you a ballpark fast.

faqs.question: How long before I can use a new driveway?
faqs.answer: Light foot traffic at 24–48 hours; vehicle traffic at 7 days minimum. We'll give you the specific timeline for your pour.

faqs.question: Why do concrete driveways crack?
faqs.answer: Usually inadequate base prep, missing control joints, or overwatering during cure. All three are addressed in our standard process.

faqs.question: Do you do stamped concrete?
faqs.answer: Yes — and we're honest that PNW conditions require sealing every 2–3 years to keep it looking good.

faqs.question: Do you handle demolition of the existing slab?
faqs.answer: Yes — demo, haul-away, and sub-base prep are included in our driveway and patio replacement process.
